channel.title: BBC News
channel.copyright: Copyright: (C) British Broadcasting Corporation, see https://www.bbc.co.uk/usingthebbc/terms-of-use/#15metadataandrssfeeds for terms and conditions of reuse.

item.title: Fewer heat-related deaths in 2025 despite warmest summer
item.description: Summer 2025 was the warmest UK summer on record, with four heatwaves, a top temperature of nearly 38C and a mean temperature of 16.1C

item.title: Doctors lose new jobs package as strike to go ahead
item.description: The offer of 1,000 more training posts has been withdrawn after the union refused to scrap the planned six-day strike.

item.title: William praises 'incredible' £20m milestone for Bowelbabe fund
item.description: The Bowelbabe fund, set up by Dame Deborah James in 2022, helps to support Cancer Research UK.

item.title: Weight-loss jabs will be offered on NHS for people at risk of further heart attacks
item.description: More than a million people in England will start being offered the anti-obesity jab for better heart health and to avoid strokes.

item.title: Review finds 250 patients need repeat bone scans
item.description: Review finds care for some patients was "below the level that would have been expected".

item.title: My daughter has childhood dementia and may not live past 16
item.description: Diagnosed just before her fourth birthday, Sophia, now 15, can no longer speak and cannot walk unaided.

item.title: Newly qualified paramedics told to apply for jobs abroad due to hire freeze
item.description: The Welsh Ambulance Service said newly qualified paramedics would not be offered roles this year due to "financial and operational issues".

item.title: 'Something wasn't right': Wrong sperm given to UK families by IVF clinics in northern Cyprus
item.description: Families of seven children believe the wrong sperm or egg donors were used in their IVF treatment.

item.title: Our skin is falling off and no-one can tell us why
item.description: People sharing pictures and accounts on socials of red, inflamed skin have triggered the first UK research into TSW.

item.title: The man who wants to bring human composting to the UK
item.description: Human composting is when a body placed in a sealed vessel containing organic matter turns into soil.

item.title: 'I nearly broke trying to help my partner with addiction issues'
item.description: Claire Harkin believes the experiences of people who are supporting loved ones with an addiction can be overlooked.

item.title: Our daughter battled Meningitis B despite being vaccinated in 'very rare' case
item.description: After Noa-Rose became critically ill, the four-year-old spent two weeks fighting for her life.

item.title: Screen time for under-fives should be limited to one hour a day, parents told
item.description: New government guidance also suggests children under-two should not be watching screens alone.

item.title: UK's transplant system was world-leading - now it lags behind other Western nations
item.description: The UK has failed to keep pace with the rest of the world. Can it regain its status, and how?

item.title: Hospital waited two days before raising alarm about meningitis outbreak
item.description: Experts say the wait was indefensible and possibly delayed identification of the outbreak.

item.title: Meningitis outbreak passes peak, says health agency
item.description: "We could still see cases continuing to come in, we need to keep an eye on those, the UKHSA says.

item.title: Cuba's mothers-to-be prepare to give birth in a country plunged into darkness
item.description: Two pregnant women tell the BBC's Will Grant of their hopes and fears as their nation is mired in crisis.

item.title: Streeting praises response to meningitis outbreak
item.description: Wes Streeting praises the "Herculean efforts" to protect people from the Kent meningitis outbreak.

item.title: I spent five months in a mother and baby mental health unit - here's what I want mums to know
item.description: Sofii Lewis was diagnosed with postpartum psychosis tendencies and OCD after giving birth.

item.title: Lab-grown food pipe offers new hope for young patients
item.description: UK scientists have grown fully functioning food pipes and successfully transplanted them into mini pigs, paving the way for human trials.

item.title: 'Having coeliac disease aged 9 means I'm normal with a tiny twist'
item.description: Nine-year-old Eliza's mum says she was relieved when her daughter was diagnosed with the disease.

item.title: Better NHS care might have saved 58 babies, BBC finds
item.description: There are growing calls for a statutory public inquiry into maternity services in Oxford.

item.title: Why has this meningitis outbreak spread so fast?
item.description: There have been 20 cases since the weekend in one small area of Kent - but this isn't the normal pattern, so what could have happened?

item.title: Hospital waiting lists in Wales see record drop
item.description: Wait times for planned care are falling but diagnostic and cancers waits are up, latest figures indicate.

item.title: Fears of two-tier health system as more turn to private care, says watchdog
item.description: The patient watchdog warns of two-tier service as polling shows numbers paying for care is on the rise.

item.title: Hundreds of GPs tell BBC they have never refused a fit note for mental health concerns
item.description: The number of fit notes issued has been rising, with more than 11.2m approved in England last year.

item.title: NHS tracker - are hospital waiting times improving near you?
item.description: Use our interactive tracker to see if treatment waits are getting better at your local hospital.

item.title: Why the benefit used by more than 8 million people may not be fit for the future
item.description: Can Universal Credit and the work and benefits system more generally reshape itself to meet a new reality?

item.title: NHS dentistry is rotting. Will the plan to fix it work?
item.description: As patients struggle to find NHS dentists, Labour has a plan but not everybody is convinced it will work

item.title: Are UK students at risk of more deadly meningitis outbreaks?
item.description: The worst seems to be over, but questions remain about why this happened and whether it could happen again.

item.title: Is screen time always bad and how do I manage it?
item.description: How do you manage screen time, does it have a bad impact and what steps can you take to mitigate the problem?

item.title: What are the symptoms of meningitis and how is it spread?
item.description: Two people have died following an "unpredecented" outbreak of meningitis in Kent.

item.title: Stay at home advice questioned and rules too tough - key findings from Covid report
item.description: An NHS close to collapse, patients failed and NHS staff put at risk - what you need to know.

item.title: Why is MenB vaccine not given to teenagers in UK and should they be offered it?
item.description: Students and older teens are not routinely vaccinated against the meningitis strain behind the Kent outbreak.

item.title: Our son loved the outdoors - invisible illness means he now can't walk or talk
item.description: Tomos is one of thousands with ME in Wales, with services described as “a desert” for those most affected.

item.title: How to check whether you have bowel cancer
item.description: What are the main symptoms and causes of bowel cancer, and how can you reduce your risk of getting it?

item.title: Why are fewer people donating their organs?
item.description: The number waiting for an organ is at a record high as loved ones increasingly block donations.

item.title: Prostate cancer screening: What you need to know
item.description: Everything you need to know about the decision on who should be screened for prostate cancer.

item.title: Could weight-loss jabs be behind rising gallbladder removals?
item.description: Last year, there was a 15% annual increase in the operations and surgeons want more research.

item.title: What is chickenpox and who can get an NHS vaccine?
item.description: The standard NHS childhood vaccination programme will include chickenpox from 2 January 2026.

item.title: The teenage caffeine pouch trend troubling US experts
item.description: Some social media influencers are pushing products to young gym-goers and students, health experts warn.

item.title: Sleep, exercise, hydrate - do we really need to stick to recommended daily doses?
item.description: As a study casts doubt on the daily steps maxim, we take a look at some other health benchmarks we’re often told to strive for.

item.title: Sewing group helping women in period poverty
item.description: Debi Angell says the kits they make help women and girls who can not afford period products.

item.title: 'I froze my eggs because I was born without a womb'
item.description: Betty Mukherjee talks to Naga Munchetty about living with Mayer-Rokitansky-Küster-Hauser syndrome.

item.title: 'I shampooed with raw egg to try and get pregnant'
item.description: Tired of health misinformation on social media, Barbora Gray is fighting back by sharing facts.

item.title: 'Women's health must be taken more seriously'
item.description: Dr Liz O'Riordan says it is important women seek help from professionals not "Google and Chat GPT".

item.title: I spent five months in a mother and baby mental health unit - here's what I want mums to know
item.description: Sofii Lewis was diagnosed with postpartum psychosis tendencies and OCD after giving birth.

item.title: Pupil art sessions 'bring joy' to hospice
item.description: The Hospice in the Weald in Pembury runs the sessions for people with life-limiting conditions.

item.title: Jersey's wellbeing score is below UK and France
item.description: Jersey's wellbeing score is below UK and France in survey of data from 41 countries.

item.title: 'You feel forgotten' - the reality for boxers after the fights stop
item.description: Retired boxer Stephen Smith shares how boxers can struggle to "fill the void" after hanging up their gloves.

item.title: Sculpting jaws, giving scores: Inside the world of looksmaxxing
item.description: Growing numbers of young men are going to great lengths to achieve what they see as the perfect face.

item.title: Charity hopes new HQ can bring people together
item.description: Health Connections is to renovate six floors of its new base as spaces for people to come together.

item.title: What is the UK Covid inquiry and how does it work?
item.description: The third report from the inquiry into the government's handling of the pandemic says the NHS was close to collapse.

item.title: Stay at home advice questioned and rules too tough - key findings from Covid report
item.description: An NHS close to collapse, patients failed and NHS staff put at risk - what you need to know.

item.title: Patients harmed as Covid pandemic brought NHS close to collapse, inquiry finds
item.description: Third report into the pandemic says patients and staff were failed as health service only just coped.

item.title: 'I'm still haunted that he died alone': The last voices of the Covid inquiry
item.description: Bereaved families have the final say as the Covid inquiry completes three years of public hearings.

item.title: Covid inquiry chair defends £200m cost and four-year process on final day
item.description: Baroness Heather Hallett said completing the hearings in under four years was an achievement but critics have questioned its cost.

item.title: Warning to patients ahead of next doctors' strike
item.description: The industrial action is due to start on 7 April and there is likely to be disruption to services.

item.title: Warning to patients ahead of next doctors' strike
item.description: The industrial action is due to start on 7 April and there is likely to be disruption to services.

item.title: PM gives BMA 48 hours to call-off strike or lose 1,000 training posts
item.description: Starmer sets deadline for doctors' union to call off six-day walkout due to begin after Easter.

item.title: Why are resident doctors striking and how much are they paid?
item.description: Resident doctors in England are striking between 17 and 22 December, the 14th walkout since 2023.

item.title: Union and government to restart talks on ending doctor dispute
item.description: BMA says there is window of opportunity in coming weeks to reach a settlement after 12 strikes.

item.title: Inside Health
item.description: James Gallagher finds out how a hot flush feels - and delves into a new treatment.

item.title: Inside Health
item.description: James Gallagher is joined by experts to answer key questions on the meningitis outbreak.

item.title: Inside Health
item.description: James Gallagher examines the outbreak of bacterial meningitis in Kent.

item.title: Inside Health
item.description: James visits Hackney, which has one of the lowest vaccination rates in the country.

item.title: How dads are bonding over learning to braid hair
item.description: Social club the Secret Life of Dads aims to provide a safe space for dads to bond with each other, and to learn skills to help them at home.

item.title: 'Would I have made it to 60 if my cancer symptoms weren't detected?'
item.description: Marie McGrath was diagnosed with bowel cancer at the start of 2026

item.title: 'I froze my eggs because I was born without a womb'
item.description: Betty Mukherjee talks to Naga Munchetty about living with Mayer-Rokitansky-Küster-Hauser syndrome.

item.title: 'You feel forgotten' - the reality for boxers after the fights stop
item.description: Retired boxer Stephen Smith shares how boxers can struggle to "fill the void" after hanging up their gloves.

item.title: Is a morning cup of hot water better than cold?
item.description: A GP looks at the social media trend of drinking hot water in the morning.
